page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180-lssd is a high-density server powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors using the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 dedicated vCPUs derived from 90 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 720 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is a key feature, consisting of 16 bundled NVMe SSDs totaling 6432 GB of high-speed capacity. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as Passmark CPU and memory evaluations. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 13529.71 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 345.79 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in database operations, executing 211.54 million Redis SET operations per second, and achieves a static web serving throughput of 94.32 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector workloads benefit from hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AVX-512 and VAES.

Given its massive local NVMe storage array and high-density compute resources, the c3d-standard-180-lssd is highly cost-efficient for IOPS-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and high-throughput web serving. It is well-suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and large language model inference, where it achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for medium and large models. However, the lack of dedicated GPU hardware makes it less optimal for deep learning training, relying instead on its robust CPU-based vector instructions for inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180-lssd is a high-density, storage-optimized server configured with 180 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor and 720 GB of RAM. It features a bundled local storage array of 16 NVMe SSDs totaling 6432 GB, with no GPU accelerators. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the server supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and VAES. Benchmark data places this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, achieving 211.54 million Redis SET operations per second and 94.32 GB/sec static web serving throughput. It offers high qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense deployments. This server is highly suited for large-scale database hosting, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180-lssd is a high-density server featuring 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and 6432 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180-lssd features 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and 6432 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute and storage powerhouse featuring 180 vCPUs and 6.4 TB of local NVMe SSDs for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 180 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM.
Bundled 6432 GB local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU performance.
Excellent fit for large-scale databases and Redis deployments.
Supports AVX-512 and VAES cryptographic hardware acceleration.
Capable of high-throughput CPU-based LLM inference.
